title: The Bridge That Danced Away
story: Imagine a bridge swaying like a giant jump rope! That's what happened to the Tacoma Narrows Bridge in Washington. Strong winds made it twist and bounce wildly on November 7, 1940. People called it 'Galloping Gertie' because of its crazy movements. The bridge couldn't handle the stress and dramatically collapsed into the water below. Luckily, no one was hurt, but engineers learned important lessons about building safer bridges.
activity_question: What nickname was given to the Tacoma Narrows Bridge?
follow_up_question: Why do you think it's important for engineers to learn from past mistakes when building bridges?

title: The Ghost Ship That Sailed Away
story: The Mary Celeste set sail from New York, bound for Italy. Little did anyone know, this cargo ship would become one of the ocean's greatest mysteries. On November 7, 1872, she left port fully stocked and crewed. Weeks later, sailors found her drifting at sea - completely empty. Not a soul was aboard, yet the ship was in good condition. title: Ancient Apes Walked Tall

title: Moon Mission: Surveyor 6 Takes Flight
story: Blast off! On November 7, 1967, Surveyor 6 rocketed into space, bound for the Moon. This robotic explorer was part of NASA's mission to prepare for human lunar landings. follow_up_question: What country was Mark Philippoussis born in?

title: A Star is Born: Yunjin Kim's Journey
story: Lights, camera, action! On November 7, 1973, a future star took her first breath in Seoul, South Korea. Yunjin Kim, destined to become a famous actress, was born. She would later dazzle audiences as Sun on the hit TV show Lost, bridging cultures as a South Korean-American performer. Kim's talent would shine bright, captivating viewers around the globe.
activity_question: What TV show is Yunjin Kim famous for starring in?
follow_up_question: Can you name another famous South Korean actor or actress?
